exercises.code:
txs = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
ventana = txs[-2:]
empty = []

ventana = txs[-2:]
empty = []

print(ventana)
print(len(ventana))

exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Slicing txs[-2:] toma los últimos elementos; en lista vacía devuelve [] sin error. El demo usa ultimas = txs[-3:] sobre transacciones del lote.

exercises.code:
ids = ('C001', 'C002')
try:
    ids.append('C003')
except AttributeError as e:
    print('diagnóstico: tuple no tiene append →', type(e).__name__, e)
    ids_list = list(ids)
    ids_list.append('C003')
    print('como list', ids_list)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T1-A-E3
exercises.answer:
ids = ('C001', 'C002')
try:
    ids.append('C003')
except AttributeError as e:
    print('diagnóstico: tuple no tiene append →', type(e).__name__, e)
    ids_list = list(ids)
    ids_list.append('C003')
    print('como list', ids_list)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] tuple no soporta append (AttributeError); list(ids) permite mutar una copia. Uso tuplas para ids inmutables y listas cuando necesito crecer.

exercises.code:

alias = xs
copia = xs.copy()
alias.append(3)
print('tras alias', xs, copia)
copia.append(4)
print('tras copia', xs, copia)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T1-B-E2
exercises.answer:
xs = [1, 2]
alias = xs
copia = xs.copy()
alias.append(3)
print('tras alias', xs, copia)
copia.append(4)
print('tras copia', xs, copia)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] alias = xs comparte objeto; copy() aísla la lista superficial de ints. Demo: mal = clientes alias vs bien shallow/deep.

exercises.code:
import copy
rows = [{'id': 'C1', 'tags': ['a']}]
shallow = rows.copy()
deep = copy.deepcopy(rows)
shallow[0]['tags'].append('b')
print('shallow afecta orig tags', rows)
deep[0]['tags'].append('z')
print('orig', rows)
print('deep', deep)
print('shallow[0] is rows[0]', shallow[0] is rows[0])
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T1-B-E3
exercises.answer:
import copy
rows = [{'id': 'C1', 'tags': ['a']}]
shallow = rows.copy()
deep = copy.deepcopy(rows)
shallow[0]['tags'].append('b')
print('shallow afecta orig tags', rows)
deep[0]['tags'].append('z')
print('orig', rows)
print('deep', deep)
print('shallow[0] is rows[0]', shallow[0] is rows[0])
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] copy() de la lista no aísla dicts/tags anidados; deepcopy sí. El demo contraste shallow dict() vs copy.deepcopy.

exercises.code:

print(d)
print(d['C002'])

exercises.answer:
pares = [('C001', 'Lima'), ('C002', 'Cusco')]
d = dict(pares)
print(d)
print(d['C002'])
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] dict(pares) construye lookup id→región desde pares hashables. Claves str ok; acceso d['C002'] del índice.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] get con default evita KeyError; acceso duro idx['C999'] lo lanza. Demo idx.get(cid) y missing C999.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Fusión {**defaults, **override} no pierde timeout y no muta defaults. update in-place sobre el original es el peligro a evitar.

exercises.code:

unicos = sorted(set(emails))
print(unicos)

exercises.answer:
emails = ['a@ex.com', 'b@ex.com', 'a@ex.com']
unicos = sorted(set(emails))
print(unicos)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] set deduplica emails; sorted da lista determinista de únicos. Orden no está garantizado en set sin sorted.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Intersección & y diferencia simétrica ^ sobre sets de emails de lotes. sorted para reporte determinista de contactos.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Si mismo id y payload distinto, listar conflict sin silenciar; unique guarda la primera. Demo seen/unique/conflicts del dedup de filas.

exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Cuento contactos anidados con len por cliente del store sintético. Demo store con contacts y txs por cliente.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Aplano txs anidadas conservando client_id en cada fila plana. Doble for/comprehension del modelo anidado S06.

exercises.code:
clients = [
    {'id': 'C001', 'txs': []},
    {'id': 'C002'},
    {'id': 'C003', 'txs': 'oops'},
]
for c in clients:
    txs = c.get('txs')
    ok = isinstance(txs, list)
    print(c['id'], 'ok' if ok else 'review')
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T3-A-E3
exercises.answer:
clients = [
    {'id': 'C001', 'txs': []},
    {'id': 'C002'},
    {'id': 'C003', 'txs': 'oops'},
]
for c in clients:
    txs = c.get('txs')
    ok = isinstance(txs, list)
    print(c['id'], 'ok' if ok else 'review')
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Shape inconsistente: falta txs o no es list → status review. No asumo claves siempre presentes en el JSON anidado.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] dig/get_nested recorre path y devuelve default si falta un nivel dict. Demo dig(c, 'profile', 'phone') sobre clientes sintéticos.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Missing = ausente o None; no confundo falsy vacío con ausencia de email. Reporte id: missing|present del nested profile.

exercises.code:
vals = [None, '', 0, []]
for v in vals:
    print(repr(v), 'falsy=', not bool(v), 'missing=', v is None)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T3-B-E3
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] 0 y '' son falsy pero pueden ser datos válidos; solo None = missing aquí. Separar truthiness de semántica de negocio del paquete.

exercises.code:
rows = [{'id': 'T2', 'monto': 30}, {'id': 'T1', 'monto': 10}]
ordered = sorted(rows, key=lambda r: r['monto'])
print([r['id'] for r in ordered])
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T4-A-E1
exercises.answer:
rows = [{'id': 'T2', 'monto': 30}, {'id': 'T1', 'monto': 10}]
ordered = sorted(rows, key=lambda r: r['monto'])
print([r['id'] for r in ordered])
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] sorted + key=lambda monto ordena sin mutar la lista original. Ids en orden T1 luego T2.

exercises.code:

    {'nombre': 'Zed', 'region': 'Lima'},

exercises.code:
rows = [3, 1, 2]
base = [3, 1, 2]
ret = rows.sort()
copy_sorted = sorted(base)
print('ret', ret, type(ret))
print('rows mutada', rows)
print('base intacta', base, 'copy_sorted', copy_sorted)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T4-A-E3
exercises.answer:
rows = [3, 1, 2]
base = [3, 1, 2]
ret = rows.sort()
copy_sorted = sorted(base)
print('ret', ret, type(ret))
print('rows mutada', rows)
print('base intacta', base, 'copy_sorted', copy_sorted)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] list.sort retorna None y muta in-place; sorted devuelve nueva lista. Selfcheck: rows.sort(key=...) retorna None.

exercises.code:
jobs = [
    'cola de llegada de filas',
    'lookup frecuente por id',
    'emails únicos de un lote',
]
structs = ['list', 'dict', 'set']
for j, s in zip(jobs, structs):
    print(f'{j} → {s}')
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T4-B-E1
exercises.answer:
jobs = [
    'cola de llegada de filas',
    'lookup frecuente por id',
    'emails únicos de un lote',
]
structs = ['list', 'dict', 'set']
for j, s in zip(jobs, structs):
    print(f'{j} → {s}')
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Cola→list, lookup id→dict, emails únicos→set: elección de estructura por job. Tradeoff del tema colecciones S06.

exercises.code:
import json
payload = {'z': 1, 'a': 2, 'ids': ['C002', 'C001']}
payload['ids'] = sorted(payload['ids'])
s = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)
print(s)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T4-B-E2
exercises.answer:
import json
payload = {'z': 1, 'a': 2, 'ids': ['C002', 'C001']}
payload['ids'] = sorted(payload['ids'])
s = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)
print(s)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] json.dumps sort_keys=True y ids sorted dan export determinista/reproducible. Demo dumps igual a y b con sort_keys.

exercises.code:
ids = ['C001', 'C002', 'C003', 'C004', 'C005']
s = set(ids)
print('list membership: O(n) por chequeo')
print('set membership: O(1) promedio')
print('C003 in set', 'C003' in s)
print('para 1000 ids, set evita escaneos lineales repetidos')
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S06 S06-T4-B-E3
exercises.answer:
ids = ['C001', 'C002', 'C003', 'C004', 'C005']
s = set(ids)
print('list membership: O(n) por chequeo')
print('set membership: O(1) promedio')
print('C003 in set', 'C003' in s)
print('para 1000 ids, set evita escaneos lineales repetidos')
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Membership en list es O(n); set O(1) promedio — tradeoff para 1000 ids. Micro-demo n=5 con x in set True.

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] xs[-2:] con xs=[1,2,3,4] produce [3,4], los dos últimos por slicing negativo. Ventana de cola del paquete de colecciones.
[D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] b=a en listas crea alias; b.append muta también a. Demo mal=clientes y score compartido.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Listar conflicto en conflicts sin silenciar
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] Mismo id y payload distinto: reportar conflicts sin borrar en silencio. dedup_report unique+conflicts del demo.
[D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] rows.sort(key=...) retorna None y muta la lista in-place. Usar sorted si necesitas una copia.
[D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True) estabiliza el orden de claves del export. Ayuda a salidas deterministas del pipeline.
